Control Arm - N.evidence: On page 1, in the 'Findings' section, it states: '1199 patients were randomly assigned to either the abiraterone acetate plus prednisone group (n=597) or placebo group (n=602).' The placebo group represents the control arm, with 602 participants.

Treatment Arm - N.evidence: On page 1, in the 'Methods' section, it states: '1199 patients were randomly assigned to either the abiraterone acetate plus prednisone group (n=597) or placebo group (n=602).' The abiraterone acetate plus prednisone group is the experimental arm. This is confirmed by Figure 1: Trial profile on page 5, which shows '597 randomly assigned to receive abiraterone acetate and prednisone plus ADT (intention-to-treat population)'.

Median Follow-Up Duration (mo).evidence: On page 1, under 'Findings', it states: 'This final analysis (data cutoff Aug 15, 2018) was done after a median follow-up of 51.8 months (IQR 47-2-57.0)'. This is reiterated on page 7, under 'Results': '...with a median follow-up of 51.8 months (IQR 47.2-57.0)...'.

Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.value: abiraterone acetate (1000 mg) once daily orally plus prednisone (5 mg) once daily orally and ADT
Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.evidence: The 'Methods' section on page 1 states: 'Patients were randomly assigned (1:1) to receive abiraterone acetate (1000 mg) once daily orally plus prednisone (5 mg) once daily orally and ADT (abiraterone acetate plus prednisone group) or matching placebos plus ADT (placebo group); each treatment cycle was 28 days.' This describes the specific regimen for Treatment Arm 1.

Control Arm.evidence: The 'Methods' section on page 1 states: 'Patients were randomly assigned (1:1) to receive abiraterone acetate (1000 mg) once daily orally plus prednisone (5 mg) once daily orally and ADT (abiraterone acetate plus prednisone group) or matching placebos plus ADT (placebo group); each treatment cycle was 28 days.' The placebo group serves as the comparison group not receiving the experimental treatment.

Treatment Arm(s).value: Abiraterone acetate plus prednisone plus ADT
Treatment Arm(s).evidence: The 'Methods' section on page 1 states: 'Patients were randomly assigned (1:1) to receive abiraterone acetate (1000 mg) once daily orally plus prednisone (5 mg) once daily orally and ADT (abiraterone acetate plus prednisone group) or matching placebos plus ADT (placebo group)'. Table 1 and Table 3 also clearly label the experimental arm as 'Abiraterone acetate and prednisone plus ADT'.

Quality of Life reported.evidence: On page 3, under 'Articles', the text states: 'In addition to the survival benefits, abiraterone acetate plus prednisone treatment showed clinical benefits in patient-reported outcomes, including pain and fatigue symptoms, and overall health-related quality of life.' This indicates that quality of life was reported.

Total Participants - N.evidence: On page 1, under 'Methods', it states: '1199 patients were randomly assigned to either the abiraterone acetate plus prednisone group (n=597) or placebo group (n=602).' The total number of participants is the sum of these two groups: 597 + 602 = 1199. This is also confirmed by Figure 1: Trial profile, which shows '1199 enrolled' and then split into the two groups.

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Trea.evidence: The 'Results' section on page 8 states: 'Median treatment duration was 25.8 months (IQR 12-3-49.0) with abiraterone acetate plus prednisone'.

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Cont.evidence: The 'Results' section on page 8 states: 'Median treatment duration was ... 14.4 months (7.3-25.8) with placebo'.

Number of Arms Included.evidence: The 'Methods' section on page 1 states: "Patients were randomly assigned (1:1) to receive abiraterone acetate (1000 mg) once daily orally plus prednisone (5 mg) once daily orally and ADT (abiraterone acetate plus prednisone group) or matching placebos plus ADT (placebo group)". This indicates two treatment arms.

Trial.evidence: The name of the clinical study is explicitly mentioned in the title: "Abiraterone acetate plus prednisone in patients with newly diagnosed high-risk metastatic castration-sensitive prostate cancer (LATITUDE): final overall survival analysis of a randomised, double-blind, phase 3 trial" on page 1. It is also referenced multiple times throughout the document, for example, in the 'Summary' and 'Methods' sections.

NCT.evidence: Found in the "Methods" section on page 1: "This study is registered at ClinicalTrials.gov, number NCT01715285".

PubMed ID.evidence: The PubMed ID is not explicitly reported in the document.

Trial Name.evidence: Found in the title of the article on page 1: "Abiraterone acetate plus prednisone in patients with newly diagnosed high-risk metastatic castration-sensitive prostate cancer (LATITUDE)".

Author.evidence: Found in the author list on page 1: "Karim Fizazi, NamPhuong Tran, Luis Fein, Nobuaki Matsubara, Alfredo Rodriguez-Antolin, Boris Y Alekseev, Mustafa Özgüroğlu, Dingwei Ye, Susan Feyerabend, Andrew Protheroe, Giri Sulur, Yesenia Luna, Susan Li, Suneel Mundle, Kim N Chi".

Year.evidence: Found on page 1, under "Published Online April 12, 2019" and in the journal citation "Lancet Oncol 2019".

Full Pub or Abstract.evidence: The document is presented as an "Article" in "Lancet Oncol", indicating a full publication rather than just an abstract.

Phase.evidence: Found in the title and "Methods" section on page 1: "randomised, double-blind, phase 3 trial".

Original/Follow Up.evidence: The title of the article is "final overall survival analysis" and the summary states "Here, we present long-term survival outcomes and safety of abiraterone acetate plus prednisone and ADT from the final analysis of the LATITUDE study", indicating a follow-up analysis.

Median Age (years) | Treatment.evidence: Table 1: Baseline characteristics on page 6 reports the mean age (SD) for the treatment arm as 67.3 (8.5) years, but the median age is not explicitly stated.

Median Age (years) | Control.evidence: Table 1: Baseline characteristics on page 6 reports the mean age (SD) for the control arm as 66.8 (8.7) years, but the median age is not explicitly stated.

Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.value: Androgen biosynthesis inhibitor (abiraterone acetate), corticosteroid (prednisone), and hormonal therapy (androgen deprivation therapy)
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.evidence: The 'Methods' section on page 1 states that patients in the first treatment arm received 'abiraterone acetate (1000 mg) once daily orally plus prednisone (5 mg) once daily orally and ADT'. The 'Introduction' section on page 2 describes abiraterone acetate as a 'selective irreversible inhibitor of the key enzyme cytochrome P17A1 that is required in androgen biosynthesis'. Prednisone is a corticosteroid, and ADT is a form of hormonal therapy.
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.group_name: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1

Quality of Life Scale.evidence: The document mentions 'patient-reported outcome measures, such as EuroQol five-dimensions, five-levels questionnaire, Brief Pain Inventory-Short Form, Brief Fatigue Inventory, and Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy Prostate scale, version 4', but explicitly states that these 'were not analysed in this final analysis' (page 4, Outcomes section). Therefore, no measure of participants' overall well-being and satisfaction from these scales is reported in this document.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Treat.evidence: Found in Table 2: 'Life-extending subsequent therapy for prostate cancer in the intention-to-treat population' on page 6, under the 'Abiraterone acetate and prednisone plus ADT (n=597)' column, row 'Docetaxel'.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Contr.evidence: Found in Table 2: 'Life-extending subsequent therapy for prostate cancer in the intention-to-treat population' on page 6, under the 'Placebos plus ADT (n=602)' column, row 'Docetaxel'.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | S.evidence: The study focuses on 'newly diagnosed high-risk metastatic castration-sensitive prostate cancer (mCSPC)' (Page 1, Summary and Methods). This inherently means synchronous metastases. Furthermore, Figure 3 'Subgroup analysis of overall survival' on page 8 includes 'Visceral disease Yes/No' as a prognostic group, which refers to synchronous metastases at randomisation.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | M.evidence: The study explicitly states it enrolled patients with 'newly diagnosed' prostate cancer with metastases (Page 1, Methods; Page 3, Study design and participants), indicating a focus on synchronous disease. There is no mention of reporting results based on metachronous disease.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | H.evidence: Page 6, 'A post-hoc analysis was done to analyse overall survival and radiographic progression-free survival on the basis of disease volume (high vs low). High-volume disease was defined as presence of visceral metastases or four or more bone metastases, with at least one outside the vertebral column or pelvis.' Page 10 further details the results of this 'post-hoc exploratory analysis of the high-volume disease subgroup'.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | L.evidence: Page 6, 'A post-hoc analysis was done to analyse overall survival and radiographic progression-free survival on the basis of disease volume (high vs low). ... Disease patterns not meeting that criteria were defined as low-volume disease.' Page 10 further details the results for the 'low-volume disease subgroup'.

Mode of metastases - N (%) | Synchronous.evidence: The 'Methods' section on page 1 and 'Study design and participants' on page 3 state that eligible patients had 'newly diagnosed... prostate cancer with metastases' and 'newly diagnosed (in the 3 months before randomisation) and histologically or cytologically confirmed adenocarcinoma of the prostate... with distant metastatic disease'. This indicates that all enrolled patients had synchronous metastases. The total number of patients in the abiraterone acetate plus prednisone group (treatment arm) was 597, as per 'Table 1: Baseline characteristics' on page 6.

The 'Methods' section on page 1 and 'Study design and participants' on page 3 state that eligible patients had 'newly diagnosed... prostate cancer with metastases' and 'newly diagnosed (in the 3 months before randomisation) and histologically or cytologically confirmed adenocarcinoma of the prostate... with distant metastatic disease'. This indicates that all enrolled patients had synchronous metastases. The total number of patients in the placebos plus ADT group (control arm) was 602, as per 'Table 1: Baseline characteristics' on page 6.

Mode of metastases - N (%) | Metachronou.evidence: The study enrolled patients with 'newly diagnosed' metastatic disease, meaning metastases were present at the time of diagnosis (synchronous). The document does not report the number or percentage of participants with metachronous metastases (metastases occurring after diagnosis) as a baseline characteristic for the treatment arm.

The study enrolled patients with 'newly diagnosed' metastatic disease, meaning metastases were present at the time of diagnosis (synchronous). The document does not report the number or percentage of participants with metachronous metastases (metastases occurring after diagnosis) as a baseline characteristic for the control arm.

Primary Endpoint(s).value: overall survival (time from randomisation to death from any cause) and radiographic progression-free survival (time from randomisation to occurrence of radiographic progression, based on the modified Prostate Cancer Working Group 2 criteria or RECIST, version 1.1)
Primary Endpoint(s).evidence: On page 1, in the 'Methods' section, it states: 'The coprimary endpoint of overall survival was assessed in the intention-to-treat population.' On page 4, under the 'Outcomes' section, it further specifies: 'The coprimary endpoints of the study were overall survival (time from randomisation to death from any cause) and radiographic progression-free survival (time from randomisation to occurrence of radiographic progression, based on the modified Prostate Cancer Working Group 2 criteria or RECIST, version 1.1) and were not centrally reviewed.'

Gleason score - N (%) | ≤ 7 | Treatment.evidence: Found in Table 1: 'Baseline characteristics' on page 6, under 'Gleason score at initial diagnosis' for 'Abiraterone acetate and prednisone plus ADT (n=597)'. Summed values for '<7' (4 [1%]) and '7' (9 [2%]) to get 13 (3%).

Gleason score - N (%) | ≤ 7 | Control.evidence: Found in Table 1: 'Baseline characteristics' on page 6, under 'Gleason score at initial diagnosis' for 'Placebos plus ADT (n=602)'. Summed values for '<7' (1 [<1%]) and '7' (15 [2%]) to get 16 (2%).

Gleason score - N (%) | ≥ 8 | Treatment.evidence: Found in Table 1: 'Baseline characteristics' on page 6, under 'Gleason score at initial diagnosis' for 'Abiraterone acetate and prednisone plus ADT (n=597)', row '≥8'.

Gleason score - N (%) | ≥ 8 | Control.evidence: Found in Table 1: 'Baseline characteristics' on page 6, under 'Gleason score at initial diagnosis' for 'Placebos plus ADT (n=602)', row '≥8'.

PS - N (%) | 0 | Treatment.evidence: Table 1 'Baseline characteristics' on page 6 reports ECOG performance status as '0-1' and '2', but does not provide a separate breakdown for PS 0.

PS - N (%) | 0 | Control.evidence: Table 1 'Baseline characteristics' on page 6 reports ECOG performance status as '0-1' and '2', but does not provide a separate breakdown for PS 0.

PS - N (%) | 1-2 | Treatment.evidence: Table 1 'Baseline characteristics' on page 6 reports ECOG performance status as '0-1' and '2'. It does not provide a separate breakdown for PS 1, which is needed to calculate '1-2'.

PS - N (%) | 1-2 | Control.evidence: Table 1 'Baseline characteristics' on page 6 reports ECOG performance status as '0-1' and '2'. It does not provide a separate breakdown for PS 1, which is needed to calculate '1-2'.

Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Treat.evidence: Found in the 'Discussion' section on page 10, which states 'high-volume disease subgroup (n=487 in the abiraterone acetate plus prednisone... group)'. The total number of patients in the abiraterone acetate plus prednisone group is 597 (from Table 1 on page 6). Percentage calculated as (487/597)*100 = 81.6%.

Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Contr.evidence: Found in the 'Discussion' section on page 10, which states 'high-volume disease subgroup (...468 in the placebo group)'. The total number of patients in the placebo group is 602 (from Table 1 on page 6). Percentage calculated as (468/602)*100 = 77.7%.

Volume of disease - N (%) | Low | Contro.evidence: Found in the 'Discussion' section on page 10, which states 'low-volume disease in this study (...n=133 in the placebo group)'. The total number of patients in the placebo group is 602 (from Table 1 on page 6). Percentage calculated as (133/602)*100 = 22.1%.

Secondary Endpoint(s).value: time to initiation of chemotherapy (time from randomisation to initiation of chemotherapy for prostate cancer), time to pain progression (time from randomisation to first increase of ≥30% from baseline in Brief Pain Inventory-Short Form [item 3]), time to next symptomatic skeletal event (time from randomisation to any one of the following skeletal-related events: clinical or pathological fracture, spinal cord compression, palliative radiotherapy to bone, or surgery to bone), time to subsequent prostate cancer therapy (time from randomisation to initiation of any subsequent therapy for prostate cancer, including hormonal therapy, chemotherapy, surgery, or radio-therapy), time to prostate-specific antigen progression (time to progression by Prostate Cancer Working Group 3 criteria), and safety results.
Secondary Endpoint(s).evidence: The secondary endpoints are explicitly listed in the 'Outcomes' section on page 4 of the document: 'We also report the secondary endpoints, namely time to initiation of chemotherapy (time from random-isation to initiation of chemotherapy for prostate cancer), time to pain progression (time from randomisation to first increase of ≥30% from baseline in Brief Pain Inventory-Short Form [item 3]), time to next symptomatic skeletal event (time from randomisation to any one of the following skeletal-related events: clinical or pathological fracture, spinal cord compression, palliative radiotherapy to bone, or surgery to bone), time to subsequent prostate cancer therapy (time from randomisation to initiation of any subsequent therapy for prostate cancer, including hormonal therapy, chemotherapy, surgery, or radio-therapy), time to prostate-specific antigen progression (time to progression by Prostate Cancer Working Group 3 criteria), and safety results.'

Add-on Treatment.evidence: The 'Summary' section states: 'the addition of abiraterone acetate plus prednisone to androgen deprivation therapy (ADT) led to a significant improvement'. The 'Methods' section further clarifies: 'Patients were randomly assigned (1:1) to receive abiraterone acetate (1000 mg) once daily orally plus prednisone (5 mg) once daily orally and ADT (abiraterone acetate plus prednisone group) or matching placebos plus ADT (placebo group)'. This indicates that abiraterone acetate plus prednisone is the additional therapy given in combination with ADT.

COE_RCT_IND_OVERALL_RJ.evidence: The document describes the results of a specific randomized controlled trial (LATITUDE study) and its findings regarding abiraterone acetate plus prednisone. It does not provide a general 'overall risk judgment of individual criteria of evidence (COE) in randomized controlled trials (RCT)'.

Treatment Class.value: Abiraterone acetate plus prednisone and ADT; Placebos plus ADT
Treatment Class.evidence: The 'Methods' section on page 1 states: 'Patients were randomly assigned (1:1) to receive abiraterone acetate (1000 mg) once daily orally plus prednisone (5 mg) once daily orally and ADT (abiraterone acetate plus prednisone group) or matching placebos plus ADT (placebo group)'.

ORR - N (%) | Treatment | Overall.evidence: The document focuses on overall survival and radiographic progression-free survival. It does not report the Overall Response Rate (ORR) as N (%) for the treatment arm.

ORR - N (%) | Treatment | CR.evidence: The document focuses on overall survival and radiographic progression-free survival. It does not report the Complete Response (CR) as N (%) for the treatment arm.

ORR - N (%) | Treatment | SD.evidence: The document focuses on overall survival and radiographic progression-free survival. It does not report Stable Disease (SD) as N (%) for the treatment arm.

ORR - N (%) | Treatment | PD.evidence: While Figure 1 (Trial profile) on page 5 reports patients who 'discontinued' due to 'progressive disease' (209 + 29 + 16 = 254 patients out of 597, or 42.5%), this is a reason for discontinuation, not an explicit reporting of Progressive Disease (PD) as an outcome of metastatic disease response (N (%)) in a response table. Therefore, the specific requested metric is not found.

ORR - N (%) | Control | Overall.evidence: The document focuses on overall survival and radiographic progression-free survival. It does not report the Overall Response Rate (ORR) as N (%) for the control arm.

ORR - N (%) | Control | CR.evidence: The document focuses on overall survival and radiographic progression-free survival. It does not report the Complete Response (CR) as N (%) for the control arm.

ORR - N (%) | Control | SD.evidence: The document focuses on overall survival and radiographic progression-free survival. It does not report Stable Disease (SD) as N (%) for the control arm.

ORR - N (%) | Control | PD.evidence: While Figure 1 (Trial profile) on page 5 reports patients who 'discontinued' due to 'progressive disease' (369 + 19 + 1 = 389 patients out of 602, or 64.6%), this is a reason for discontinuation, not an explicit reporting of Progressive Disease (PD) as an outcome of metastatic disease response (N (%)) in a response table. Therefore, the specific requested metric is not found.

Median OS (mo) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: On page 1, in the 'Findings' section, it states: 'Overall survival was significantly longer in the abiraterone acetate plus prednisone group (median 53.3 months [95% CI 48-2-not reached])'. This is confirmed in Table 3 on page 7 under 'Primary endpoint: Overall survival' for 'Abiraterone acetate and prednisone plus ADT'.

Median OS (mo) | Overall | Control.evidence: On page 1, in the 'Findings' section, it states: 'than in the placebo group (36.5 months [33.5-40.0])'. This is confirmed in Table 3 on page 7 under 'Primary endpoint: Overall survival' for 'Placebos plus ADT'.

Median OS (mo) | High volume | Treatment.evidence: On page 10, in the 'Discussion' section, it states: 'In our post-hoc exploratory analysis of the high-volume disease subgroup (n=487 in the abiraterone acetate plus prednisone and 468 in the placebo group), median overall survival was 49.7 months (95% CI 43.2–55.4) with abiraterone acetate plus prednisone'.

Median OS (mo) | High volume | Control.evidence: On page 10, in the 'Discussion' section, it states: 'compared with 33.3 months (30.2-36.7) with placebos'.

Median OS (mo) | Low volume | Control.evidence: On page 10, in the 'Discussion' section, it states: 'and the median overall survival in this subgroup [low-volume disease] was not reached in either study group'.

Type of Therapy.value: Participants were randomly assigned to receive abiraterone acetate (1000 mg) once daily orally plus prednisone (5 mg) once daily orally and ADT (abiraterone acetate plus prednisone group) or matching placebos plus ADT (placebo group).
Type of Therapy.evidence: The 'Methods' section on page 1 states: 'Patients were randomly assigned (1:1) to receive abiraterone acetate (1000 mg) once daily orally plus prednisone (5 mg) once daily orally and ADT (abiraterone acetate plus prednisone group) or matching placebos plus ADT (placebo group); each treatment cycle was 28 days.' This describes the two types of treatment provided to participants.

OS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: On page 7, Table 3 'Efficacy endpoints in the intention-to-treat population', under 'Primary endpoint' and 'Overall survival', the 'Abiraterone acetate and prednisone plus ADT (n=597)' group reported 275 (46%) events (deaths). The survival rate is derived as 100% - 46% = 54%.

OS Rate (%) | Overall | Control.evidence: On page 7, Table 3 'Efficacy endpoints in the intention-to-treat population', under 'Primary endpoint' and 'Overall survival', the 'Placebos plus ADT (n=602)' group reported 343 (57%) events (deaths). The survival rate is derived as 100% - 57% = 43%.

Region - N (%) | Europe | Treatment.evidence: Derived by summing the N/N values for 'Eastern Europe' (99/214) and 'Western Europe' (83/155) from Figure 3 on page 8, under the 'Abiraterone acetate and prednisone plus ADT (n/N)' column. Total N for Europe in the treatment arm is 99+83=182. The total N for the treatment arm is 597 (from 'All patients' row). Percentage is (182/597)*100 = 30.48%.

Region - N (%) | Europe | Control.evidence: Derived by summing the N/N values for 'Eastern Europe' (136/217) and 'Western Europe' (94/162) from Figure 3 on page 8, under the 'Placebos plus ADT (n/N)' column. Total N for Europe in the control arm is 136+94=230. The total N for the control arm is 602 (from 'All patients' row). Percentage is (230/602)*100 = 38.21%.

PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: The document reports median progression-free survival in months and hazard ratios for PFS, but does not explicitly state a 'PFS Rate (%)' as a single percentage value for the treatment arm. For example, on page 3, it mentions 'median radiographic progression-free survival was 33.0 months (95% CI 29.6-not reached)' and on page 7, Table 3, 'Secondary progression-free survival' is 53.3 months (44.7-58.1) with 45% events, but not a percentage rate.

PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Control.evidence: The document reports median progression-free survival in months and hazard ratios for PFS, but does not explicitly state a 'PFS Rate (%)' as a single percentage value for the control arm. For example, on page 3, it mentions 'median radiographic progression-free survival ... versus 14.8 months (14-7-18.3) in the placebo group' and on page 7, Table 3, 'Secondary progression-free survival' is 30.1 months (26.2-33.4) with 56% events, but not a percentage rate.
